channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Elon Musk says federal employees must fill out productivity reports or resign
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said Saturday that all federal employees are expected to fill out a mandatory productivity report form and will be considered resigned if they do not.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USDA launches review at University of Maine over transgender sports policy after Trump-Gov. Mills blowup
item.description: Federal agencies launched reviews into the University of Maine's Title IX policies after a tense exchange between President Trump and the state's governor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump revels in mass federal firings and jeers at Biden before adoring conservative crowd
item.description: President Donald Trump says “nobody has ever seen anything” like his administration’s sweeping effort to fire thousands of federal employees and shrink the size of government.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Many Americans don't trust the media to cover Trump: Survey
item.description: Most Americans in a new survey said they have little confidence that the news media can cover President Trump with fairness and accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: At CPAC, Argentina’s Milei explains his chainsaw methods and likens them to Musk’s DOGE
item.description: Argentina’s President Javier Milei is urging conservatives to keep supporting measures to shrink the size of government.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know as Michigan Republicans select new chair to lead party into the 2026 midterms
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Michigan Republicans are gathering in Detroit on Saturday to elect a new chair who will lead the party into the 2026 midterms in the battleground state, when several key races will be decided including a critical U.S. Senate seat.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk’s cost-cutting team is laying off workers at the auto safety agency overseeing his car company
item.description: Elon Musk’s cost-cutting team is eliminating jobs at the vehicle safety agency that oversees Tesla and has launched investigations into deadly crashes involving his company’s cars.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: National Park Service rangers laid off as DOGE makes cuts
item.description: National Park Service ranger Mikayla Moors was one of thousands of government employees laid off by the DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk blurs the line between his government and business roles
item.description: The tech titan and President Trump say they will avoid any conflicts of interest, but it’s difficult for the public to verify that.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge considering a request to further block Trump administration from freezing funds
item.description: A federal judge on Friday heard arguments on a request to further block President Donald Trump ’s administration from freezing trillions of dollars of grants and loans that fund everything from clean energy programs to bridge repairs to emergency shelters. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol on assault charge after press conference
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former Proud Boys national leader Enrique Tarrio was arrested Friday near the U.S. Capitol on a charge that he assaulted a woman protesting a gathering attended by Tarrio and others who received presidential pardons for crimes stemming from the Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate adopts budget resolution after marathon overnight vote-a-rama
item.description: The Senate adopted a budget resolution Friday intended to serve as a blueprint to deliver the first part of President Trump’s agenda. Senators voted 52-48, mostly along party lines, on the resolution after a marathon overnight voting session. Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) voted against the measure.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed $500,000 fund would help victims of investment schemes in Nevada
item.description: Victims of investment schemes would be eligible for $25,000 from a fund managed by the Nevada Secretary of State's Office under a proposal presented in Carson City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Watch live: Trump hosts event honoring Black History Month
item.description: The celebration comes amid the administration's moves to gut federal diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs and place DEI employees on leave.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month in: Survey
item.description: The CNN poll shows that while 47 percent of adults in the U.S. say they approve of Trump's moves since returning to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Senate GOP dismayed by Trump, Zelensky war of words
item.description: Public comments from President Trump blaming Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky for Russia’s invasion of his country are unnerving Senate Republicans, who have largely sought to avoid conflicts with the White House.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026
item.description: McConnell, the longest-serving Senate party leader in history, chose his birthday to share his decision not to run for another term.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A military vet and a scientist were securing America’s food system. Trump fired them
item.description: More than 10,000 federal employees who had yet to complete their probationary periods have been fired by the Trump administration, including those who work to protect American agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: A gold-framed Trump mug shot is hanging just outside the Oval Office
item.description: President Trump’s mug shot is one of the first things visitors will see when approaching the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: Latino business owners say customers are staying away in wake of deportation threats
item.description: Some Latino business owners say customers have been staying away out of fear of being detained and deported. Twin Cities business and city leaders are encouraging people to buy from Latino-owned shops this Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Medina, MPR News

item.title: Florida Democrats file bill to repeal 6-week abortion ban
item.description: House Minority Leader Fentrice Driskell, who represents families in Hillsborough County, said the legislation comes because it’s a mandate from voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump will sign new executive orders while his first joint TV interview with Musk airs in prime time
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday was set to sign new executive orders from his Florida home while his first joint TV interview with adviser Elon Musk airs in prime time.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Vice President Kamala Harris to receive NAACP's Chairman's Award
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris will receive the NAACP's Chairman's Award during the civil rights organization’s Image Awards ceremony this weekend. The Chairman's Award honors individuals who excel in public service and leverage their unique platforms to ignite and drive meaningful change.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
